title: Inspect fails for .Image field
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman run -t -d fedora:30 cat

2. podman inspect -f="{{.Image}}" 1e7c4920ed4b

3.

**Describe the results you received:**

Error: Template parsing error: template: image:1:2: executing "image" at <.Image>: can't evaluate field Image in type *libpod.InspectContainerData

**Describe the results you expected:**

The value of "Image": "9eff96f4c827f839118cd36a028c8ae00187fbda5707a470bb60f0ece860c0cf",


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.5.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.7
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
debug:
  compiler: gc
  git commit: ""
  go version: go1.12.7
  podman version: 1.5.1
host:
  BuildahVersion: 1.10.1
  Conmon:
    package: podman-1.5.1-3.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/libexec/podman/conmon
    version: 'conmon version 2.0.0, commit: d728afa06cd2df86a27f32a4692c7099a56acc97-dirty'
  Distribution:
    distribution: fedora
    version: "30"
  MemFree: 494751744
  MemTotal: 16617947136
  OCIRuntime:
    package: runc-1.0.0-93.dev.gitb9b6cc6.fc30.x86_64
    path: /usr/bin/runc
    version: |-
      runc version 1.0.0-rc8+dev
      commit: e3b4c1108f7d1bf0d09ab612ea09927d9b59b4e3
      spec: 1.0.1-dev
  SwapFree: 8031039488
  SwapTotal: 8380215296
  arch: amd64
  cpus: 8
  eventlogger: journald
  hostname: laped.localdomain
  kernel: 5.2.17-200.fc30.x86_64
  os: linux
  rootless: true
  uptime: 100h 38m 32.26s (Approximately 4.17 days)

  blocked: null

  - docker.io

  ConfigFile: /home/laa/.config/containers/storage.conf
  ContainerStore:
    number: 2
  GraphDriverName: overlay
  GraphOptions:
  - overlay.mount_program=/usr/bin/fuse-overlayfs
  GraphRoot: /home/laa/.local/share/containers/storage
  GraphStatus:
    Backing Filesystem: extfs
    Native Overlay Diff: "false"
    Supports d_type: "true"
    Using metacopy: "false"
  ImageStore:
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-1.5.1-3.fc30.x86_64
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Hi @laped83, thanks for the report. I can reproduce and found the issue.

@baude @mheon, the problem is that the go field is called `ImageID` while the json identifier is called `Image` (see https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/libpod/container_inspect.go#L99). Strictly speaking, changing `ImageID` to `Image` is breaking the API. _However_, a simple `podman inspect $CTR` will print the json structure, so all users should have been tricked into trying to filter for `Image` which makes me consider it a bug fix. WDYT?

@vrothberg We actually do normalization for a few of these in the `inspect` code - when we've changed identifiers previously, we did a crude string-replace to ensure the old ones still worked - https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/cmd/podman/inspect.go#L99-L106

I think we should replace ImageID with Image, and add another one of these string-replaces as a band-aid to keep from breaking folks. We can deprecate and remove them at a later date.
